exercises.title: Text Processing Functions
exercises.description: Build a small text processing toolkit. Implement three functions: one to count words, one to count unique words, and one to find the most common word.
hints: text.split() splits on whitespace and returns a list of words.
set() removes duplicates — len(set(words)) counts unique items.
max(counts, key=...) finds the key with the highest value.

exercises.title: Combining Functions, Lambdas, and Built-ins
exercises.description: Use functions, lambdas, sorted(), enumerate(), and zip() together to produce a ranked leaderboard.
exercises.starterCode:
students = [
    {"name": "Alice", "score": 92, "subject": "Python"},
    {"name": "Bob", "score": 78, "subject": "Python"},
    {"name": "Carol", "score": 95, "subject": "Python"},
    {"name": "Dave", "score": 88, "subject": "Python"},
]

def assign_grade(score: float) -> str:
    """Return letter grade for a score."""
    if score >= 90: return "A"
    if score >= 80: return "B"
    if score >= 70: return "C"
    return "D"

# Sort by score descending
ranked = sorted(students, key=lambda s: s["score"], reverse=True)

print("=== Leaderboard ===")
for rank, student in enumerate(ranked, start=1):
    grade = assign_grade(student["score"])
    print(f"#{rank}  {student['name']:<10} {student['score']}  ({grade})")

scores_only = [s["score"] for s in students]
print(f"\nClass average: {sum(scores_only) / len(scores_only):.1f}")
print(f"High score:    {max(scores_only)}")
print(f"Low score:     {min(scores_only)}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
=== Leaderboard ===
#1  Carol      95  (A)
#2  Alice      92  (A)
#3  Dave       88  (B)
#4  Bob        78  (C)

Class average: 88.2
High score:    95
Low score:     78
hints: sorted(..., key=lambda s: s['score'], reverse=True) sorts highest first.

exercises.title: Closure-Based Validator Factory
exercises.description: Use closures to create reusable validator functions. Each validator is created by a factory function and can be called on any value.
exercises.expectedOutput:
25 is valid (range 0-120)
150 is INVALID (must be 0-120)
87 is valid (range 0-100)
'alice' is valid (length 5)
'ab' is INVALID (length 2, must be 3-20)
hints: The inner validate function captures min_val and max_val from the enclosing scope.
Each call to make_range_validator creates a new closure with different min/max values.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Course 4 Functions Summary
warmup.description: Functions, default args, lambdas, and closures in one block.
warmup.starterCode:
# Default args
def repeat(text, times=3):
    return (text + " ") * times

print(repeat("Go"))
print(repeat("Yes", 2))

# Lambda + sorted
words = ["banana", "fig", "apple", "cherry"]
print(sorted(words, key=len))

# Closure
def make_adder(n):
    return lambda x: x + n

add10 = make_adder(10)
print(add10(5))
print(add10(20))
